summary: Test suite covers the three primary bump scenarios (feat→minor, fix→patch, breaking→major) across both version file formats (plain text and package.json) with working fixtures and integration validation through the GitHub Actions workflow. However, critical gaps undermine production readiness: (1) Zero error-path testing despite task requirement for 'handling errors gracefully' — no tests for invalid version formats, missing files, malformed commits, or invalid JSON; (2) No edge cases (empty commits, mixed commit types, priority ordering, BREAKING CHANGE footer variant, unrecognized types); (3) No unit-level tests of individual functions (Read-Version, Get-BumpType, Step-Version, etc.) — all integration-only through act; (4) No evidence of TDD methodology (test-first); (5) Brittle regex-based assertions on act output that would break if logging changes; (6) Changelog format validation incomplete (date not mocked, only structure checked). Workflow validation and fixture structure are well-executed, but test depth is shallow. Suite would catch happy-path regressions but miss error handling bugs and boundary conditions critical for production scripts.
